drugs.notes: Flat 500mg/m² dose given every 2 weeks (days 1 and 15 of 28-day cycle). First infusion over 120 minutes; if well tolerated subsequent infusions may be given over 60 minutes. Rate must not exceed 10mg/min. Dose banded per national dose bands (5mg/ml). Monitor for hypersensitivity for 60 minutes after infusion.
300mg once daily orally days 1-28. Available as 50mg and 75mg capsules. If encorafenib is permanently discontinued, cetuximab should also be discontinued and vice versa.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. Neither cetuximab nor encorafenib are myelosuppressive; no dose reduction needed for haematological toxicity.
Reduce encorafenib dose to 300mg (1st reduction level). Not recommended in severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh Class C).
Withhold cetuximab until transaminases ≤5xULN and bilirubin ≤1.5xULN.
Administer only where serum creatinine is 1.5xULN or below.
No data available; use with caution in severe renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Liver toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 2 - no improvement within 4 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Maintain encorafenib. If no improvement within 4 weeks, withhold until improved to grade 0/1 or baseline, then resume at same dose.
Withhold for up to 4 weeks. If improved to grade 0/1, resume at reduced dose (225mg/day). If not improved, permanently discontinue.
Consider permanently discontinuing encorafenib.
Withhold for up to 4 weeks. If improved to grade 0/1, resume at reduced dose. If not improved, permanently discontinue.
Permanently discontinue encorafenib.
Reduce to 225mg once daily.
Reduce to 150mg once daily.

1st reduction: 400mg/m². 2nd reduction: 300mg/m². Dose reductions are permanent. Note: cetuximab dose reductions are generally NOT permitted; only in context of concurrent encorafenib dose reduction or grade 3 skin toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 3 (first occurrence, resolves ≤14 days)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until toxicity resolves to grade ≤2. If resolves within 14 days, resume at same dose.
Delay up to 14 days with concomitant dose reduction. Dose reductions are permanent.

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity: >2 consecutive infusions withheld, 4th grade 3 episode, or any grade 4
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue cetuximab. If cetuximab discontinued, encorafenib must also be discontinued.
Reduce infusion rate by 50% (total infusion must not exceed 240 minutes).
Stop infusion, administer supportive therapy. Once resolved to grade ≤1, resume at 50% of previous rate.
Stop infusion immediately. Do not re-administer cetuximab.
Withhold encorafenib. Resume at reduced dose when QTcF ≤500ms. Discontinue if more than one recurrence.

dose_modifications.condition: Uveitis grade 2 or 3 improving to grade 0/1
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold and repeat ophthalmic monitoring within 2 weeks. If improved to grade 0/1, resume at reduced dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Uveitis grade 4 or not improved within 6 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue encorafenib.
Withhold until improved to grade 0/1, then resume at same dose.
Withhold until improved to grade 0/1, then resume at reduced dose.
Permanently discontinue encorafenib.
Stop treatment permanently.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
H2 antagonist according to local formulary (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral TDS when required (take home, day 1 cycle 1 only)
Doxycycline 100mg oral once daily for 28 days (prophylaxis for skin rash)
special_warnings: BRAF V600E mutation and RAS wild type must be confirmed before starting therapy.
Cetuximab dose reductions are NOT routinely permitted; any reduction is permanent.
If encorafenib is permanently discontinued, cetuximab must also be discontinued and vice versa.
Monitor serum electrolytes (magnesium, potassium, calcium) — correct abnormalities to reduce QT prolongation risk.
Acniform skin rash occurs in >70% of patients receiving cetuximab; onset normally within 3 weeks.
Encorafenib associated with QTc prolongation — correct electrolytes and control risk factors before starting.
Haemorrhagic events in 21% of patients on encorafenib — monitor haemoglobin, epistaxis, blood in stool/urine.
New or worsening cutaneous malignancies (cuSCC) may occur with BRAF inhibitors — instruct patients to report new skin lesions.
Uveitis including iritis and iridocyclitis can occur with encorafenib — assess at every visit.
Monitor for hypersensitivity for 60 minutes after cetuximab infusion. Do not administer other chemotherapy during this period.
Formal medical review should occur by end of first 8 weeks of treatment.
WHO performance status 0 or 1 required.
UV radiation may worsen cetuximab skin reactions — sun safety practices for duration of treatment and 2 months after.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 Day 1 loading dose: 400mg/m² over 120 minutes. Rate must not exceed 5mg/min for first infusion. Dose banded per national dose bands (5mg/ml). An interval of 60 minutes should be left between cetuximab and oxaliplatin.

Cycle 1 Day 8 maintenance dose: 250mg/m² over 60 minutes. Rate must not exceed 10mg/min for subsequent infusions. | NOTE: Two Cetuximab 250mg/m² day 8 entries exist — one for cycle 1 day 8 only and one for cycle 2 onwards day 8. Both are intentional and represent the same maintenance dose on day 8 for different cycles.
Cycle 2 onwards Day 1 maintenance dose: 250mg/m² over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (5mg/ml).

Cycle 2 onwards Day 8 maintenance dose: 250mg/m² over 60 minutes. | NOTE: Two Cetuximab 250mg/m² day 8 entries exist — one for cycle 1 day 8 only and one for cycle 2 onwards day 8. Both are intentional and represent the same maintenance dose on day 8 for different cycles.
In 500ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. Glucose 5% flush before and after. Dose banded per national dose bands (5mg/ml). Central venous access required.
In 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. May be replaced with calcium levofolinate 175mg IV in 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. No dose adjustment needed for haematological counts.
IV bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (50mg/ml).
Continuous IV infusion over 46 hours via ambulatory pump. Central venous access required. Dose banded per national dose bands (50mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophils <1.5x10⁹/L or platelets <75x10⁹/L - delay up to 7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Delay and reinitiate at full dose for up to 7-day delay.
Reinitiate at 75% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutropenia persisting >7 days — omit bolus fluorouracil
dose_modifications.notes: Omit the bolus fluorouracil dose for this and subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: further delay needed despite omitting bolus 5-FU
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce infusional fluorouracil to 80% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: further delay needed despite omitting bolus 5-FU
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce oxaliplatin to 80% of original dose.
Stop therapy.

dose_modifications.condition: Liver impairment: bilirubin >85 μmol/L or AST/ALT >180 units
dose_modifications.notes: Fluorouracil contra-indicated.
Reduce initial dose by 33% in moderate hepatic impairment.
Reduce initial dose by 50% in severe hepatic impairment. Dose may be increased as tolerated.
Unlikely to require dose adjustment but administer only when within these thresholds.
Dose reduce for CrCl <20ml/min. For moderate impairment, treat at normal dose and monitor renal function, adjusting according to toxicity.
Consider dose adjustment in severe renal impairment.
Withhold fluorouracil until resolved to grade ≤1, then restart at full dose.
Delay treatment; restart at 75% of original dose once resolved to grade ≤1.
Restart fluorouracil at 50% of original dose.
Withhold until resolved to grade ≤1, then restart at 75% of original dose.
Wait until resolved to grade ≤1, then resume at 50% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Diarrhoea NCI-CTC Grade 3 (third occurrence) or Grade 4 at any time
dose_modifications.notes: Stop fluorouracil therapy permanently.
Administer full dose oxaliplatin.
Reduce oxaliplatin to 75mg/m².
Discontinue oxaliplatin.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3-4 diarrhoea or stomatitis recurring despite fluorouracil dose reduction
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce oxaliplatin to 75mg/m².

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 3 (first occurrence, resolves ≤14 days)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until toxicity resolves to grade ≤2. If resolves within 14 days, resume at same dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 3 (2nd occurrence, dose reduction to 200mg/m²)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay up to 14 days; reduce to 200mg/m². Dose reduction is permanent.

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 3 (3rd occurrence, dose reduction to 150mg/m²)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay up to 14 days; reduce to 150mg/m². Dose reduction is permanent.

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity: >2 consecutive infusions withheld or 4th episode of grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue cetuximab.
Reduce infusion rate by 50%.
Stop infusion, administer supportive therapy. Resume at 50% of previous rate once resolved to grade ≤1.
Stop immediately; do not re-administer.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV bolus (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Dexamethasone 8mg oral (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
H2 antagonist according to local formulary (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1 only)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral twice daily for 3 days (take home, starting day 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral TDS when required (take home)
Loperamide 4mg after first loose stool then 2-4mg four times daily PRN (max 16mg/24h)
special_warnings: DPD testing required before initiation: patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity from fluorouracil.
Cetuximab dose reductions are permanent and NOT routinely permitted.
Central venous access and ambulatory infusion pump required for 46-hour fluorouracil infusion.
An interval of 60 minutes must be left between cetuximab and oxaliplatin administration.
Monitor for hypersensitivity for 60 minutes after cetuximab infusion; do not administer other chemotherapy during this period.
Acniform skin rash in >70% of patients receiving cetuximab; onset normally within 3 weeks.
Serum electrolytes (magnesium, potassium, calcium) should be monitored and corrected.
Peripheral neuropathy with oxaliplatin is cumulative — acute laryngopharyngeal dysaesthesia may increase with infusion duration.
Rare cases of acute interstitial lung disease or lung fibrosis with oxaliplatin — stop for unexplained respiratory symptoms.
Folinic acid may be replaced with calcium levofolinate 175mg IV in 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes.
Response to treatment is often assessed after 6 cycles.
WHO performance status 0 or 1 required.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 Day 1 loading dose: 400mg/m² over 120 minutes. Rate must not exceed 10mg/min. An interval of 60 minutes should be left between cetuximab and irinotecan.
Cycle 1 Days 8 and 15 maintenance dose: 250mg/m² over 60 minutes.

Cycles 2-6 Days 1, 8, 15: 250mg/m² over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (5mg/ml). | MAINTENANCE DOSE — Cycles 2-6 Days 1, 8, 15: this entry covers cycle 2 onwards. A separate entry covers cycle 1 maintenance (days 8 and 15 only at 250mg/m²). The loading dose entry (400mg/m² cycle 1 day 1) is a distinct entry.

In 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 90 minutes. May be administered over 30-90 minutes. Maximum daily dose 700mg. Dose banded per national dose bands (20mg/ml). Consider starting dose of 300mg/m² (max 600mg) for patients with prior pelvic radiotherapy, age ≥70, or PS 2.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, restart irinotecan at 80% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: 14-day delay required or two separate 7-day delays
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce irinotecan to 50% of original dose, or consider stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: nadir neutrophils <0.5x10⁹/L or febrile neutropenia
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce subsequent irinotecan dose to 80% of original dose.
Reduce to 200mg/m² (approx. 57% of 350mg/m² dose).
Consider stopping therapy.
Administer only when transaminases ≤5xULN and bilirubin ≤1.5xULN.
Administer only where serum creatinine is ≤1.5xULN.

Delay until symptoms resolved, then restart at 250mg/m².
Stop treatment.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay up to 14 days; reduce to 150mg/m². Dose reduction is permanent.

dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue cetuximab.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
H2 antagonist according to local formulary (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral twice daily for 3 days (take home, starting day 2 of cycle)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral TDS when required (take home)
Loperamide 4mg after first loose stool then 2-4mg four times daily PRN (max 16mg/24h)
special_warnings: DPD testing required before initiation: patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity from fluorouracil.
Confirm wild type KRAS status prior to starting therapy.
Cetuximab dose reductions are permanent and NOT routinely permitted.
Central venous access and ambulatory infusion pump required for 46-hour fluorouracil infusion.
An interval of 60 minutes must be left between cetuximab and the remaining chemotherapy.
Monitor for hypersensitivity for 60 minutes after cetuximab infusion; do not administer other chemotherapy during this period.
Avoid oxaliplatin when CrCl <30ml/min.
Peripheral neuropathy with oxaliplatin is cumulative; acute laryngopharyngeal dysaesthesia may occur.
Rare cases of acute interstitial lung disease or lung fibrosis with oxaliplatin — stop for unexplained respiratory symptoms.
Folinic acid may be replaced with calcium levofolinate 175mg IV in 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes.
Acniform skin rash in >70% of patients receiving cetuximab.
This regimen may require funding approval.
WHO performance status 0 or 1 required.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 (week 1) loading dose: 400mg/m² over 120 minutes. Rate must not exceed 10mg/min.
Cycle 2 onwards (weekly maintenance): 250mg/m² over 60 minutes. Rate must not exceed 10mg/min. Dose banded per national dose bands (5mg/m). Monitor for hypersensitivity for 60 minutes after infusion.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until levels are achieved. Decision to continue at consultant's discretion.
Administer only when transaminases ≤5xULN and bilirubin ≤1.5xULN.
Administer only where serum creatinine is ≤1.5xULN.

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 3 (first occurrence, resolves ≤14 days)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until toxicity resolves to grade ≤2. Resume at same dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 3 (2nd occurrence, dose reduction to 200mg/m²)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay up to 14 days; reduce to 200mg/m². Dose reduction is permanent.

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 3 (3rd occurrence, dose reduction to 150mg/m²)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay up to 14 days; reduce to 150mg/m². Dose reduction is permanent.

dose_modifications.condition: Skin toxicity: >2 consecutive infusions withheld, 4th grade 3 episode, or any grade 4
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue cetuximab.
Reduce infusion rate by 50% (max total 240 minutes).
Stop infusion, give supportive therapy; resume at 50% of previous rate when resolved to grade ≤1.
Stop immediately; do not re-administer.
Stop treatment.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
H2 antagonist according to local formulary (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to cetuximab)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral TDS when required (take home, day 1 cycle 1 only)
special_warnings: Confirm wild type KRAS status prior to starting therapy.
Cetuximab dose reductions are permanent and NOT routinely permitted.
This is a weekly cetuximab schedule: loading dose 400mg/m² (cycle 1) then maintenance 250mg/m² weekly (cycle 2 onwards).
Monitor for hypersensitivity for 60 minutes after cetuximab infusion; do not administer other chemotherapy during this period.
Acniform skin rash in >70% of patients receiving cetuximab; onset normally within 3 weeks.
Serum electrolytes (magnesium, potassium) should be monitored and corrected.
UV radiation may worsen skin reactions — sun safety for duration and 2 months after.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 cycle 1 and every 6-8 weeks thereafter.
This regimen may require funding approval.
WHO performance status 0, 1, or 2 required.
